The following matters were heard: PCN-12-295329 Conservatorship of Jack Michael Sargent and
PTR-14-297706 The Jack Michael Sargent Special Needs Trust.

Appearances:
David Monsour for and with Co-Conservators/Co-Trustees, Neal Sam Sargent and Carol Nelson.
Mercy Hall former Counsel for Co-Conservators/Co-Trustees, Neal Sam Sargent and Carol Nelson.

The Court noted the 4th accounting has been filed. Mr. Monsour stated the 4th accounting petition is set for Dec. 3, 2019 covering period of Nov. 2016 - July 2019, that they are not ready for the final accounting, will get Letters issued today from the clerk's office to get funds released, and hope to submit the final accounting by the end of this year. Mr. Sargent stated they have concluded domestic court (Quadro), need to get funds from T. Rowe Price to distribute to ex-wife and issue of transferring funds to the Trust for dependent child (age 21) until she is age 25.

The Third and Final Account and Report of Conservators; etc., is continued to December 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the 4th accounting petition. The Court notes the 4th accounting may be amended to make it final accounting if appropriate.

The Status on Transferring Funds to the Trust, and Status on Filing Bond are continued to December 3, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the related conservatorship matter.

The Order to Appear are taken off Calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mercy Hall for (trustees) Neal Sargent & Carol Nelson.

Ms. Hall addressed her non-appearance on 2/2/16 stating that they thought the matters had been coordinated to one date, that the February 2, 2016 date did not get on their calendar and that they have had issues with notices going to old addresses. Ms. Hall addressed the bond stated that they do not have the final numbers on the family law action, that the assets were not moved into the Special Needs Trust (SNT), that they are trying to finalize what should be moved over, that they may need to petition to reduce the bond amount and that the QDRO is not complete yet. The Court asked if there are assets available to be transferred to the SNT. Ms. Hall responded that they are in the conservatorship, that there is a small amount of cash and a larger amount in retirement accounts and that there was an order to transfer partial funds to the trust but that is was not done. The Court asked if there is still no date as to when to expect resolution from the family law court. Ms. Hall stated that it is up to the parties to push forward, that the trustees are the same parties as the conservators, that it is not an issue with the conservators and that the ex-wife is devastated over the death of Mr. Sargent. The Court stated that it was given the same reports when he was alive, that they need to get in compliance with the court order and that if there is no urgency for the parties in the family law case it is not helping anyone. Ms. Hall stated that she is not sure what power her client has to push to get something done. The Court stated that presumably the family court issued an order expecting it to be carried out
and finalized, that this has been going on for two years now, that something needs to happen, that the court will continue the status hearing and will expand it to status on transferring assets into the trust and adjustment of bond, that at the next status hearing it wants a report from the trustees as to why the family court matter is not done and what they can do or need to get it done. Ms. Hall stated that she will speak with the family law attorney. The Status of filing bond was continued to April 12,2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the status on transferring assets into the trust and adjustment of bond was set for the same. The Order to Appear is off calendar.
